commit.message:
Globalize prompts: embed defaults in binary, store in ~/.yumemory/prompts/

- Move prompt storage from workspace/_yumem/prompts/ to ~/.yumemory/prompts/
- Embed all default prompts in binary using go:embed (internal/prompts/defaults/)
- PromptManager.Initialize() writes embedded defaults to ~/.yumemory/prompts/
  only if files don't already exist (preserves user edits)
- Remove workspace dependency from prompts package
- Remove writeDefaultPromptTemplates() and analyzeContentPrompt from workspace.go
- Add prompt initialization to `yumem init` command

add Cursor and Claude config files to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add dotnet network allowlist to NuGet update workflow (#30)

## Summary

- The NuGet update workflow's Copilot agent was getting **403 errors**
from the Squid proxy when reaching `api.nuget.org`
- NuGet domains weren't in the `network` allowlist — the workflow only
had `network: defaults`
- Added the `dotnet` ecosystem identifier to allow access to
NuGet/dotnet package registry domains

## Root cause

From the [first run
logs](https://github.com/chrisjainsley/Augustus/actions/runs/22803445365):

> "I cannot complete the NuGet package update task due to network
restrictions blocking access to the NuGet API (api.nuget.org). The
environment's firewall returns a 403 error when attempting to connect to
NuGet services."

## Change

```yaml
network:
  allowed:
    - defaults
    - dotnet    # <-- added
```

## Test plan

- [ ] Merge and re-run `NuGet Package Update` workflow with `dry_run:
true`
- [ ] Verify the agent can reach NuGet and list outdated packages

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Fix: add Mihomo install + TPROXY fail-safe, fix WiFi/DNS after reset

- Add setup-clash.sh: installs Mihomo binary, geo files, kernel modules
- Add clash-init.sh: /etc/init.d/clash with FAIL-SAFE — TPROXY nft rules

  force DHCP option 6=192.168.1.1 so WiFi clients use AGH as DNS
- Fix wifi-optimize.sh: enable disabled SSIDs after factory reset
- Rename adguardhome/adguardhome.yaml -> adguardhome/config.yaml
- Update install.sh: run setup-clash.sh + setup-adguardhome.sh in sequence

Root cause of original failure: nftables TPROXY rules loaded at boot but
Mihomo failed to start → all traffic blackholed. Fixed by conditional apply.

commit.message:
docs: changelog rule — every commit, no exceptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make facts.valid_at nullable with NULL default for property facts [bu-axm.9] (#488)

* fix: make facts.valid_at nullable with NULL default for property facts [bu-axm.9]

The previous bitemporal implementation (mem_007) diverged from the OpenSpec
contract in module-memory/spec.md in three ways:

1. facts.valid_at was NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), so property facts could never
   truly be non-temporal — every stored fact got a timestamp even when the
   caller passed no valid_at.

2. store_fact() set fact_valid_at = valid_at if valid_at is not None else now(),
   embedding the same default-to-now() bug at the application layer.

3. Property-vs-temporal coexistence was keyed off predicate_registry.is_temporal
   instead of valid_at nullness, requiring a DB roundtrip that mis-modeled the
   storage semantics defined by the spec.

This commit fixes all three issues:

Migration mem_008:
- Alters facts.valid_at to TIMESTAMPTZ nullable DEFAULT NULL.
- Resets existing rows where valid_at ≈ created_at (old DEFAULT now()
  artifact) back to NULL so they become proper property facts.
- Recreates the three partial uniqueness indexes filtered to valid_at IS NULL,
  so they enforce uniqueness only among property facts; temporal facts coexist
  freely.

storage.py:
- fact_valid_at = valid_at (None when omitted — property fact, datetime when
  provided — temporal fact).
- skip_supersession = fact_valid_at is not None: temporal facts always skip
  supersession regardless of predicate_registry.is_temporal.
- All supersession lookup queries now include AND valid_at IS NULL to
  target only property facts, so temporal facts are never accidentally
  superseded by a new property fact with the same key.
- Removes the predicate_registry fetchval call from the supersession path
  (no longer needed).

Tests updated/added:
- test_valid_at_defaults_to_null_when_omitted: property facts store NULL
- test_temporal_fact_skips_supersession: valid_at != None → no supersession
- test_property_fact_still_supersedes: valid_at == None → supersession applies
- test_predicate_registry_not_queried_for_supersession: no registry lookup
- test_supersession_sql_filters_valid_at_null: query has AND valid_at IS NULL
- test_property_fact_does_not_supersede_temporal_fact: coexistence
- test_temporal_fact_does_not_supersede_property_fact: coexistence
- Updated entity_id and object_entity_id tests to reflect new fetchval counts
  (entity check only, no is_temporal call) and NULL valid_at expectation.
- Updated chain registration test to include mem_008.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct downgrade() comment: back-fill uses created_at not now()

The comment on line 102 stated "NULL → now()" but the implementation
correctly uses created_at. Using created_at is the right choice since it
preserves a deterministic, existing timestamp rather than stamping with
current wall-clock time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct mem_008 docstring and spec for temporal-fact uniqueness gap

The migration docstring incorrectly claimed the new partial indexes would
catch same-key same-valid_at temporal fact duplicates. All three indexes
carry AND valid_at IS NULL, so temporal facts (valid_at IS NOT NULL) have
NO DB-level uniqueness constraint. The comment now accurately states this
and notes that application-layer deduplication is required.

Also update openspec/specs/module-memory/spec.md lines 836-839 to
reflect the AND valid_at IS NULL WHERE condition on all three partial
unique indexes, and note that temporal facts rely on app-layer dedup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(BUG-1477): remove redundant tombstone calls, drop conflicting trigger

The tasks table already has `trg_task_tombstone` (BEFORE DELETE) that
auto-creates tombstones. The app's manual recordTombstone() call and
our new AFTER INSERT trigger on tombstones were redundant and caused
PostgreSQL error 27000 (same-command tuple modification conflict).

- Removed recordTombstone() from permanentlyDeleteTask (DB trigger handles it)
- Migration now drops the redundant trigger instead of creating it

Extends meal_log() to accept and persist the optional contextual metadata
fields defined in the butler-health spec: mood_before (1-10), satisfaction
(1-10), symptom_notes (TEXT), and tags (ARRAY of dietary markers).

Fields are persisted in the fact metadata JSONB only when provided, so
existing callers that omit them are fully backward-compatible. _fact_to_meal
surfaces them in the meal API shape when present, returning None for absent
fields. The MCP tool registration in tools.py is updated with matching
optional parameters and docstring.

Tests cover: all-fields persistence, per-field storage in store_fact kwargs,
omitted-fields absent from metadata, partial-field subsets, and
meal_history round-trip retrieval of stored contextual metadata.

Acesso individual para cada garcom: nome + toque para entrar

Antes: todos garcons compartilhavam uma unica conta por empresa.
Agora: cada garcom tem seu proprio acesso individual.

Fluxo do garcom:
1. Tela de login > aba Garcom > seleciona empresa
2. Ve lista dos garcons cadastrados com nome e avatar
3. Toca no seu nome > entra direto (sem senha)

Fluxo do admin:
- Configuracoes > Usuarios > "Novo Garcom" > digita o nome > pronto
- Garcons aparecem separados dos admins na lista

Tecnico:
- API /api/auth/garcons: lista garcons por empresa
- API /api/auth/garcons/create: admin cadastra novo garcom
- API /api/auth/garcom: login individual com garcomId
- Cada garcom tem sua propria conta Supabase Auth
- Compatibilidade mantida com conta compartilhada como fallback

commit.message:
add WebGPU fused Plan7+transducer GPU kernels (batch and single-sequence)

Two WGSL compute shaders for GPU-accelerated fused Plan7 Forward/Viterbi:
- Batch kernel: B sequences in parallel, one workgroup per sequence
- Single-sequence kernel: K threads per position with workgroup barriers
Both use f32 log-space arithmetic with logsumexp/maxplus semiring selection.

New API: fusedForwardBatch(), fusedViterbiBatch() for database search.
Existing fusedForward()/fusedViterbi() now auto-use GPU when available.
createFusedPlan7() accepts backend option for WebGPU detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: per-template capability sandbox (#222) (#279)

## Summary

Adds three new optional fields to `AgentTemplate` for declarative
per-template sandboxing:

- **`shell_allowlist`** — restrict which shell binaries the agent can
execute (Strict mode). Applied to ShellTool, CustomTool, AND PluginTool
via a shared `ShellSecurityConfig` built once at kernel boot.
- **`max_token_budget`** — per-agent-run token cap. Resolved via
`min(template, global)` so templates can restrict but never expand
beyond global.
- **`max_tool_calls`** — hard cap on total tool calls across the entire
agent run via a dedicated `ToolCallLimitTracker` (AtomicU32).
Intentionally NOT wired into LoopGuard.

Also adds **TOML template loading** — `.toml` files in the template
directory are now parsed alongside `.json`.

All fields are optional with `None` defaults — zero behavior change for
existing templates.

### Key design decisions

| Decision | Choice |
|----------|--------|
| Shell enforcement scope | Shared config injected into ShellTool,
CustomTool, AND PluginTool |
| `max_tool_calls` enforcement | Dedicated atomic counter, NOT LoopGuard
(which only counts repetitions) |
| Token budget naming | `max_token_budget` (not `max_tokens` — avoids
collision with response generation field) |
| Template can restrict, not expand | `min()` for numeric limits |

### Files changed

- `src/config/templates.rs` — 3 new fields + TOML loading + 7 tests
- `src/kernel/registrar.rs` — `build_shell_config()` + ToolDeps.template
+ 4 tests
- `src/kernel/mod.rs` — pass template to ToolDeps
- `src/tools/custom.rs` — `with_security()` constructor + 1 test
- `src/tools/plugin.rs` — security field + `validate_command()` before
`sh -c` + 1 test
- `src/agent/budget.rs` — `resolve_token_budget()` + 4 tests
- `src/agent/tool_call_limit.rs` — NEW: `ToolCallLimitTracker` + 5 tests
- `src/agent/mod.rs` — module + re-export
- `src/agent/loop.rs` — tracker field + enforcement in both
streaming/non-streaming loops
- `src/config/types.rs` — `max_tool_calls` on AgentDefaults
- `src/config/mod.rs` — env var override
- `src/config/validate.rs` — known field
- `src/cli/common.rs` — template overrides for budget + tool calls

## Test plan

- [x] 3055 lib tests pass (22 new tests added)
- [x] 127 doc tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
- [x] `cargo fmt -- --check` clean
- [x] Shell allowlist: template with `["git"]` blocks `curl`; empty list
blocks all; `None` uses default
- [x] Token budget: `min(template, global)` logic verified for all 4
branches
- [x] Tool call limit: tracker increments per-call, stops at limit,
unlimited when None

Closes #222

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Per-run tool-call limits with enforcement, truncation, logging, and
final-synthesis fallback.
* Templates can specify max token budgets, max tool calls, and shell
command allowlists.
  * Token-budget resolution combines global and template overrides.
* Shell security configs applied to tools; commands validated against
allowlists.
* Templates loadable from JSON or TOML; env var to override default max
tool calls.

* **Tests**
* Comprehensive unit tests for budgeting, tool-call limits, template
parsing, and security behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

commit.message:
feat(parity): add OpenAPI, protocol, and HTTP shadow parity checks

Extends the parity harness with three new cross-language checks:

1. OpenAPI schema parity (openapi-parity.ts)
   - exports full FastAPI OpenAPI schema via export_parity_snapshot.py
   - extractRoutes resolves $ref/anyOf/allOf, maps types to canonical set
   - compareOpenApiSchemas diffs response body fields route-by-route
   - 193 routes validated from Python server

2. Protocol message parity (protocol-parity.ts)
   - exports all 18 Pydantic message class schemas via model_json_schema()
   - TS_MESSAGE_MANIFEST encodes all ProcessingMessage fields statically
   - compareProtocolMessages checks required/optional fields across 130 fields
   - Result: 0 errors, 1 known warning (BinaryUpdate bytes vs Uint8Array)

3. Live HTTP shadow testing (shadow.ts + cross-language-http.test.ts)
   - fetchJson with AbortController timeout, compareHttpResponses for object/array diff
   - cross-language-http.test.ts activates when NODETOOL_TEST_SERVER_URL is set
   - gracefully skips in CI when no server is running

Also adds error logging to UnifiedWebSocketRunner and wires resolveProvider
in test-ui-server.

118 tests passing across 16 test files.

commit.message:
fix(BUG-1477): handle concurrent delete in tombstone trigger

App deletes task row then inserts tombstone. The AFTER INSERT trigger
tried to delete the same row again, causing PostgreSQL error 27000.
Wrapped trigger DELETE in exception handler — if row is already gone,
it's a no-op.

commit.message:
fix: z-index stacking so chat area renders above orb scene

The Three.js canvas was overflowing its container and rendering on top
of the chat messages. Added overflow:hidden + z-index layering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: security hardening — D2A pubkey verification, CORS restriction, score validation, Sentry observability

- D2A sender pubkey cross-check rejects spoofed payloads
- CORS origin filter restricts http:// to localhost only
- Cache/deliver score bounds validation (finite, 0-10)
- Sentry environment tag, user context, D2A span instrumentation
- 10 silent catch blocks converted to logged errors
- Removed unnecessary defensive code and decorative comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: aegis
repository.description: This is an experimental Protocol that combines "AI + Nostr + Internet Computer" to transform individual information consumption into a zero-noise briefing, and further enable agents to exchange this information among themselves.

commit.message:
V0.3.8: Fix 9 SHOULD FIX from review

- S1: resolvePath logs stderr warning when falling back to process.cwd()
- S2: context.ts file reading parallelized (10-batch concurrency)
- S3: todo_update type-safe updates, no unsafe Record cast
- S4: server.test.ts documents SDK version dependency (^1.12.1)
- S5: detectStage uses weighted signals — CI alone no longer means launch
- S7: todo_add capped at 500 active items to prevent unbounded growth
- S8: recommend_reset shows dismissed list before clearing
- S11: prebuild uses node rmSync instead of rm -rf (cross-platform)
- S12: README clarifies AGPL — using as dev tool is free

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: user registration (surname + ORCID) and role-based access

- Users must register with surname (English) and ORCID to access the system
- User data stored permanently in cfd_users table (Supabase)
- Regular users see only "Author Dossier"
- Admin users also see "Settings" (and future pages)
- Admin ORCIDs configured via CFD_ADMIN_ORCIDS env variable
- Add auth.py module with login/register/logout flow
- Add auth translations for both ua and en locales
- Add sql/create_cfd_users.sql migration script
- Add admin_orcids field to Settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Defence.html

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(admin): calculate product stats from local data instead of missing API

The /api/admin/products/stats endpoint doesn't exist, causing stat cards
to show 0 values. Changed to calculate stats locally from the products
array using a useEffect that updates when products change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update Promo Gajian package with complete details

- Change name from "Paket Premium" to "Promo Gajian"
- Add complete package details based on OCR from service-3.jpg:
  * Silver: Rp 184.000 (Body Massage + Refleksi + Head + Totok Wajah)
  * Gold: Rp 289.000 (Body Massage + Scrub + Head + Totok Wajah + Manicure/Pedicure)
  * Bronze: Rp 325.000 (Body Massage + Scrub + Head + Totok Wajah + Eyelash)
- All packages include 120 min treatment duration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
